item.title: Anthropic Restores Claude Fable 5 Access and Rolls Out Claude Sonnet 5
item.content:encoded:
Anthropic to restore Claude Fable access on Wednesday — May Read

Anthropic says the U.S. Department of Commerce has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, and it will begin restoring Fable 5 access on Wednesday. Mythos 5 will remain limited to select companies, and it is still unclear whether Fable 5 access will be restored globally.

  • Anthropic confirmed that the Department of Commerce has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
  • Anthropic said it will begin restoring access to Claude Fable 5 on Wednesday.
  • Claude Mythos 5 will remain exclusive to select companies.
  • Anthropic said it is grateful to users for their patience and to those who worked on redeploying the models.
  • The article says it is unclear whether Fable 5 will roll out to everyone, including users outside the United States.

Anthropic rolls out Sonnet 5 with near-Opus 4.8 performance at a lower price — Optional

Anthropic is rolling out Claude Sonnet 5, a cheaper model that brings agentic planning, tool use, coding, and research capabilities much closer to its flagship Opus 4.8. It is available to Free, Pro, and Max users, with introductory API pricing below Opus.

  • Anthropic is rolling out Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper model that is intended to perform close to its flagship Opus 4.8 model.
  • Anthropic says Sonnet 5 is built to be its most agentic Sonnet model yet, with capabilities such as planning and using tools like browsers and terminals.
  • The article says Sonnet 5 is a major upgrade for coding, research, automation, document work, and other multi-step tasks.
  • Anthropic says Sonnet 5 will launch with introductory API pricing of $2 per million input tokens and $10 per million output tokens through August 31, 2026, rising afterward to $3 and $15 respectively.
  • Sonnet 5 is available to users on Free, Pro, and Max subscriptions.
